### Newsletters

## 1. [Weekly AI Digest] What's Shipping in AI

**Publication/Publisher:** Chan's Weekly AI Newsletter
**Publication date:** Apr 6, 2026
**Author:** Chan Meng (author)
**URL:** <https://chanmeng.org/#newsletter>

```
A weekly newsletter I write — every Monday, curated picks from the hottest new AI tools on Product Hunt, condensed into a three-minute read that keeps subscribers current on what's trending in AI.

Free, with a five-second subscribe. It's the lightweight, tools-focused counterpart to the deeper research breakdowns in Decoding the Future.

### Press features about Chan

## 2. Meet the FemTech Founders Redefining Women's Health at Work

**Publication/Publisher:** THISDAYLIVE
**Publication date:** May 28, 2025
**Author:** THISDAYLIVE editorial — Chan Meng featured
**URL:** <https://www.thisdaylive.com/2025/05/28/meet-the-femtech-founders-redefining-womens-health-at-work/>

```
A press feature profiling the founding team behind Sanicle and its mission to redefine women's health in the workplace, published during my tenure as CTO at Sanicle.

The article situates Sanicle's work within the wider FemTech movement — building technology that treats women's workplace health as core infrastructure rather than an afterthought — and features the people driving it. Being profiled here put the platform's engineering and product vision in front of an international readership focused on health innovation.
```

### Hosted podcasts

## 3. [Podcast — host] Decoding the Future

**Publication date:** May 4, 2025

**URL:** <https://open.spotify.com/show/0PDYYnZhKwV7PdWHeK7nbj>

```
An English-language podcast I host, exploring the landmark research papers driving innovation in Artificial Intelligence, Large Language Models, and beyond — translating dense research into something a working engineer or curious listener can actually use.

The show runs alongside a companion LinkedIn newsletter of the same name, with 800+ subscribers, so each episode reaches both audio and reading audiences.

## 4. [Podcast — host] Future Turing 未来图灵

**Publication/Publisher:** Spotify
**Publication date:** May 4, 2025
**Author:** Chan Meng (host)
**URL:** <https://open.spotify.com/show/201m2sZ5VNAEGKRBzf2ZZ4>

```
The Chinese-language counterpart to Decoding the Future — a podcast I host that decodes landmark research papers in Artificial Intelligence, LLMs, and other cutting-edge fields for a Chinese audience.

The framing question of the show: "Where is the boundary of intelligence? How will the future be shaped by code?" Running parallel English and Chinese editions lets the same research reach listeners across both language communities.
```

### Podcast guest appearances

## 5. [Podcast] From Math Teacher to FemTech CTO: Chan Meng on AI and Women's Health Innovation

**Publication/Publisher:** Femtech At Work
**Publication date:** Apr 22, 2025
**Author:** Femtech At Work (host) — Chan Meng (guest)
**URL:** <https://open.spotify.com/episode/0aNgDy0Qw6oQMXNxhszZv2>

```
Greater China Edition, Episode 21 of the Femtech At Work podcast — a conversation about my path into FemTech and how AI is being used to understand and improve women's health.

What the episode covers:
• My journey from teaching mathematics to becoming a CTO in tech — proof you can follow a non-linear path into the field
• Where the real innovations are happening in women's health technology
• How AI is being applied to women's health, and the considerations that come with it — data privacy chief among them
• The specific challenges of discussing and addressing women's health in a Chinese cultural context
• Down-to-earth advice for anyone thinking about starting their own venture in the women's-health space
```

---

### Press features about Chan

## 6. AI-Driven Health Solutions for Women in the Workplace — UN CSW69 & Columbia University SIPA

**Publication/Publisher:** FemTech Weekend
**Publication date:** Apr 2, 2025
**Author:** FemTech Weekend editorial — Chan Meng featured
**URL:** <https://chanmeng.org/blog/from-code-to-care-un>

```
A feature in FemTech Weekend's publication on my work as an independent developer presenting at the UN Commission on the Status of Women (CSW69) and Columbia University SIPA's international forum on technology and women's health.

I presented "Sani," an AI health assistant that delivers personalised health recommendations for women in the workplace. The solution pairs a privacy-first architecture — protecting employee data while still surfacing actionable insight — with a dual-view dashboard that serves both employees and HR teams.

The presentation drew direct response from senior figures:
• IBM representatives expressed interest in a pilot collaboration
• The Minister of Gender and Children's Affairs from Sierra Leone praised it as the first comprehensive solution connecting menstrual-cycle data to actionable workplace policy through AI analysis

The feature reflects a consistent approach to FemTech: minimalist architecture and end-to-end full-stack delivery in service of women's health — "Less is More" applied to a hard problem.
```

### Podcast guest appearances

## 7. From Math Teacher to Full-Stack Developer

**Publication/Publisher:** 小宇宙FM — 码农姐妹 (Xiaoyuzhou FM, "Coder Sisters")
**Publication date:** Feb 22, 2025
**Author:** 码农姐妹 (host) — Chan Meng (guest)
**URL:** <https://www.xiaoyuzhoufm.com/episode/67b2082405a90dfd0ddbcaa2>

```
A guest appearance on 《码农姐妹》(Coder Sisters), a podcast from Xiaoyuzhou FM, where I shared my career transition from teaching maths to working as a full-stack developer.

We also talked about my minimalist lifestyle and my observations on the job markets in China, the US, and New Zealand, and I explained how I use AI to learn programming and how it accelerated my move into engineering — a practical look at retraining into tech rather than an abstract one.
```

### Press features about Chan

## 8. Weekly Pulse: Remote MCP, Images, Screen Recording

**Publication/Publisher:** PulseMCP
**Publication date:** Jan 4, 2025
**Author:** Tadas Antanavicius (PulseMCP) — Chan Meng's MCP servers featured
**URL:** <https://www.pulsemcp.com/posts/newsletter-remote-mcp-images-screen-recording>

```
Third-party coverage in the PulseMCP newsletter, where the Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers I built — server-google-news and server-google-jobs — were featured among the week's notable ecosystem contributions. MCP is Anthropic's open standard that lets AI agents safely use real tools and data.

Being picked up by an independent MCP-ecosystem newsletter, written by Tadas Antanavicius, is external validation of an early contribution to the agent-tooling space — the servers were useful enough that a curator chose to surface them to a specialist audience.
```

### Minimalism / lifestyle press

## 9. Minimalist to the End, I Threw Away My Old Name

**Publication/Publisher:** NetEase — Kan Ke inSight (网易看客 inSight)
**Publication date:** Oct 18, 2024
**Author:** Chan Meng
**URL:** <https://chanmeng.org/blog/threw-away-my-old-name>
**Engagement:** 29,000+ reads · 534 audio listens · 569 likes · 1,118 shares · 136 bookmarks · 106 comments

```
A personal narrative on radical minimalism that goes well beyond ordinary decluttering — showing how extreme simplification of possessions, relationships, and daily habits can drive genuine personal transformation. Drawing on firsthand experience, it offers practical insight into living minimally in modern life, from an empty room to reimagined social connections and even personal identity.

Engagement: 29,000+ reads · 534 audio listens · 569 likes · 1,118 shares · 136 bookmarks · 106 comments.
```
